opportunities.title: [OSS] clarvia-org/workflow-web: Add ARIA labels to navigation and interactive elements
opportunities.description:
## What needs doing

The website needs better accessibility. Screen readers rely on ARIA labels to describe interactive elements.

## Steps
1. Run the dev server: `npm run dev`
2. Audit the landing page for elements missing `aria-label`, `aria-describedby`, or `role` attributes
3. Focus on: navigation links, form inputs, buttons, language switcher
4. Add appropriate ARIA attributes
5. Test with a screen reader or browser accessibility tools (Chrome DevTools → Accessibility tab)
6. Open a PR

## Time estimate
~30 minutes

## Skills needed
Basic HTML/React knowledge. Familiarity with web accessi

opportunities.title: [OSS] clarvia-org/workflow-web: Add a 'Contribute' page linking to open issues
opportunities.description:
## What needs doing

We need a page on the website that tells visitors how they can help — and links directly to our GitHub issues.

## What the page should include
- A brief explanation of what Clarvia is and why contributions matter
- Types of help needed: source verification, translation, accessibility, documentation
- A direct link to issues labeled `good first issue`
- A link to our CONTRIBUTING.md
- Clear statement that non-coders are welcome

## Where
Create a new page at `/[lang]/contribute` with trilingual support.

## Time estimate
~45 minutes

## Skills needed
React/Next.js, basic i

opportunities.title: [OSS] clarvia-org/workflow-web: Add GitHub organization link to website footer
opportunities.description:
## What needs doing

The live website at clarvia.org should link back to our GitHub organization so visitors can find the source code and contribute.

## Steps
1. Find the footer component in the codebase
2. Add a link to `https://github.com/clarvia-org` with appropriate icon or text
3. Keep it consistent with the existing footer design
4. Test on all three languages (EN/FR/DE) to make sure nothing breaks
5. Open a PR

## Time estimate
~20 minutes

## Skills needed
Basic React/Next.js and CSS.

opportunities.title: [OSS] Priyanshu-byte-coder/devtrack: [GOOD FIRST ISSUE] Add aria-labels to time range buttons for accessibility
opportunities.description:
## Problem

The time range buttons in `ContributionGraph` and `TopRepos` render text like `"7d"`, `"14d"`, `"30d"` — too cryptic for screen readers. They also lack `aria-pressed` to indicate the currently selected range.

## Expected Behavior

Screen readers announce buttons as *"Show 7-day range"*, *"Show 30-day range"*, etc. The active button is announced as pressed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Add `aria-label={`Show ${days}-day range`}` to each time range button in `ContributionGraph.tsx`
- [ ] Add `aria-label={`Show ${r.days}-day range`}` to each time range button in `TopRepos.tsx`
- [

opportunities.title: [OSS] Priyanshu-byte-coder/devtrack: [GOOD FIRST ISSUE] Add error state to PRMetrics, StreakTracker, and TopRepos
opportunities.description:
## Problem

Three components silently fail on API errors: `PRMetrics.tsx`, `StreakTracker.tsx`, and `TopRepos.tsx`. Each uses `.catch(() => {})` so users see nothing when data fails to load — no error message, no retry prompt.

## Expected Behavior

Each component shows a user-friendly error message when its API call fails, instead of staying blank or frozen in a loading state.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Add `error` state (`useState<string | null>(null)`) to each of the three components
- [ ] Populate `error` in the `.catch()` block of each `fetch` call
- [ ] Render an error message in pla

opportunities.title: [OSS] Priyanshu-byte-coder/devtrack: [GOOD FIRST ISSUE] Add error state to ContributionGraph
opportunities.description:
## Problem

`ContributionGraph.tsx` silently swallows fetch errors — `.catch(() => {})` — so users see a blank chart with no explanation when the API call fails.

## Expected Behavior

When the API call fails, show a friendly error message inside the chart card (e.g. a red-bordered box: *"Failed to load contribution data. Please try refreshing."*).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Add `error` state: `const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)`
- [ ] In the `.catch()` block, call `setError("Failed to load contribution data.")`
- [ ] Render error UI conditionally (instead of chart) wh

opportunities.title: [OSS] Aashikhandelwal05/PrepIQ: [Feature]:  [GSSoC] Improve empty states and onboarding UX across dashboard modules
opportunities.description:
### Problem statement

 Problem

Several sections across the platform currently display visually minimal empty states when no user data is available, including:

* Dashboard
* Mock Interview
* Job Tracker
* Interview Prep

The current experience feels static and provides limited onboarding guidance for first-time users.

Proposed Improvements

* Add richer empty state UI components
* Improve onboarding guidance and CTA visibility
* Add contextual illustrations/icons
* Improve spacing and visual hierarchy
* Add quick-action suggestions for first-time users

opportunities.title: [OSS] Priyanshu-byte-coder/devtrack: [GOOD FIRST ISSUE] Add 'copy to clipboard' button to streak stats
opportunities.description:
## Problem

Users want to share their streak stats (e.g. in a README or social post) but have to manually read and copy numbers. A one-click copy button would improve shareability.

## Expected Behavior

A small clipboard icon button appears in the `StreakTracker` card header. Clicking it copies a pre-formatted text summary to the clipboard, e.g.:

```
🔥 DevTrack Stats
Current streak: 14 days
Longest streak: 21 days
Active days: 87
```

A brief "Copied!" confirmation replaces the icon for ~2 seconds after clicking.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Add a clipboard icon button (use an emoji `📋` or

opportunities.title: Manager, Security Incident Response Team (USA)
opportunities.description:
<div class="content-intro"><p>GitLab is the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps. GitLab enables organizations to increase developer productivity, improve operational efficiency, reduce security and compliance risk, and accelerate digital transformation. More than 50 million registered users and more than 50% of the Fortune 100* trust GitLab to ship better, more secure software faster.</p>
<p>The same principles built into our products are reflected in how our team works: we embrace AI as a core productivity multiplier, with all team members expect
